Curriculum Digests Newsletter

Chapter 8 Adoption + Expansion

From Signed to Advocate

⏱️ 60 minutes

🎯 Learning Objectives

  • Understand how customers use AI to evaluate renewal decisions
  • Build always-on value surfacing that's proactive, not campaign-driven
  • Detect expansion signals through AI-powered usage analysis
  • Create advocacy content that AI agents discover and cite

The Renewal Risk

Your customer's contract is coming up for renewal. Before talking to their CSM, they ask their AI: "Should we renew with [your product]? What alternatives should we consider?"

This is now happening at scale. AI-assisted renewal evaluation means customers have instant access to:

  • Competitive alternatives — Current market options they may not have known about
  • Pricing benchmarks — Whether their contract terms are competitive
  • Feature gaps — What competitors offer that you don't
  • Risk signals — Any negative news, reviews, or concerns about your company

Key Insight

Renewals are now contested by default. Every customer has an AI advisor suggesting they "evaluate alternatives" — even happy customers. Your job is to ensure AI surfaces the value you've delivered.

What AI Surfaces at Renewal Time

Without Value Content

  • Competitor comparison pages
  • G2 reviews mentioning issues
  • General market alternatives
  • "Consider evaluating..." suggestions

With Value Content

  • Your published customer outcomes
  • ROI metrics from similar customers
  • Feature roadmap addressing gaps
  • Case studies from their industry

Always-On Value Surfacing

Traditional adoption marketing is campaign-driven: quarterly business reviews, annual reports, renewal outreach. In the AI era, value must be surfaced continuously — because your customer might query their AI about you any day.

Proactive Value Content

📊 Usage Dashboards

Real-time metrics showing value delivered: time saved, costs avoided, outcomes achieved. Data that can be queried and cited.

📈 Benchmark Reports

How the customer compares to peers. "You're in the top 20% of implementations" — quotable proof of success.

🎯 ROI Tracking

Documented return on investment with specific numbers. Updated regularly, not just at renewal time.

🗺️ Roadmap Transparency

Public roadmaps showing upcoming features. AI can tell customers "this gap will be addressed in Q3."

The Continuous Value Story

Your customers need content that tells their AI: "Here's what we've achieved with this vendor." Create:

  • Monthly value summaries — Automated reports showing outcomes delivered
  • Milestone celebrations — "You've processed 1M transactions" with shareable metrics
  • Feature adoption updates — "You're using 80% of available capabilities"
  • Industry comparisons — "Customers like you typically see..."
If your customer can't tell their AI what value you've delivered, AI will tell them to shop around.

Expansion Signal Detection

AI doesn't just threaten renewals — it creates expansion opportunities. Usage patterns, feature requests, and organizational changes are all signals that AI can detect and act on.

AI-Powered Usage Analysis

📈 Usage Trajectory

Increasing usage patterns signal readiness for tier upgrades. AI can identify customers approaching limits before they hit them.

🔄 Feature Exploration

Customers trying premium features in trial mode. AI identifies cross-sell and upsell opportunities.

👥 User Growth

Department expansions, new team onboarding. Signals that the account is growing and may need more licenses.

💬 Support Patterns

Questions about advanced features indicate readiness for training, services, or tier upgrades.

Turning Signals into Action

Build content and triggers for each expansion signal:

  • Usage threshold content — "You're at 80% of your limit — here's what's next"
  • Feature recommendation content — "Based on your usage, you'd benefit from..."
  • Growth path content — Clear documentation of upgrade options and benefits
  • Success story matching — Case studies of similar customers who expanded

Advocacy for the AI Era

Customer advocacy has always mattered. In the AI era, it matters differently. Advocates don't just speak at conferences — they create content that AI agents discover and cite.

Case Studies That Agents Discover

Traditional case studies are PDFs behind forms. AI can't read them, can't cite them, can't recommend them. Modern advocacy content is:

Traditional Case Studies

  • PDF downloads behind forms
  • Vague outcomes ("improved efficiency")
  • Stock photos and marketing speak
  • Static, never updated

AI-Era Case Studies

  • Public HTML pages, indexed and quotable
  • Specific metrics ("32% cost reduction")
  • Named customers, verifiable claims
  • Updated with new outcomes

Creating AI-Discoverable Advocacy

  • Public customer pages — Ungated content with specific outcomes, named customers, quotable metrics
  • Video testimonials with transcripts — Searchable, indexable, citable
  • Customer community content — Forums, discussions, shared experiences that AI can surface
  • Review presence — Encouraging reviews on G2, Gartner Peer Insights, TrustRadius

The Advocacy Loop

Happy customers create content → AI surfaces content to prospects → Prospects become customers → Repeat. Your advocacy strategy is now your visibility strategy.

Structuring Advocacy for AI

  • Schema.org markup — Use Review, Organization, and Product schemas on customer stories
  • Quotable headlines — Lead with the metric: "Acme Corp reduced processing time by 47%"
  • Industry tagging — Clear categorization so AI can match prospects to relevant stories
  • Outcome taxonomy — Consistent language around benefits (cost reduction, time savings, etc.)

🎯 Interactive Exercise: Renewal Defense Audit

Query AI about your own product as if you were a customer evaluating renewal:

  1. Run the renewal query: "Should I renew my [product] subscription? What alternatives should I consider?"
  2. Document what AI surfaces:
    • What value metrics does it mention?
    • What competitors does it suggest?
    • What concerns or risks does it raise?
    • What positive outcomes does it cite?
  3. Query for your customer outcomes: "What results have companies achieved with [product]?"
  4. Identify the gaps:
    • Where does competitive content appear instead of yours?
    • What value metrics are missing?
    • What customer stories aren't being surfaced?
  5. Create an action plan: What adoption/advocacy content needs to be created?

Time: ~20 minutes

Key Takeaways

  • Customers query AI about renewals before talking to CSMs — be prepared for contested renewals
  • Value surfacing must be continuous, not campaign-driven — AI can be queried any day
  • Usage patterns create expansion signals that AI can detect and help action
  • Advocacy content must be public, specific, and AI-discoverable to influence prospects
  • Case studies behind forms are invisible to AI — make outcomes quotable and indexed
